title: 破镜谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\137-破镜谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
During a conversation with Heather and Arthur Badcock, Miss Marple asserts that the murder previously associated with Gossington Hall did not actually occur there, but rather the body was moved to that location. Heather discusses her admiration for the actress Marina Gregg, recounting a time she met the star despite being ill. During the exchange, Miss Marple observes that Heather reminds her of a woman named Eleanor Wilder, later mentioning to Heather that her friend Alison Wilder is dead. Arthur Badcock also recalls a memory regarding the loss of antique Georgian teaspoons that Heather had once given to a family from a farmhouse.

In a separate scene, Miss Marple visits Mrs. Bantry at her small house near the east gate of Gossington Hall. Mrs. Bantry explains that she sold the main Hall and much of its land following the death of her husband, Colonel Bantry, noting the property's history of failed ownership, including periods as a hotel and its division into apartments. The two women discuss Marina Gregg, who has purchased Gossington Hall with her husband, a film producer or director. Mrs. Bantry notes that Marina's career declined following a breakdown after her divorce. Miss Marple shares her observations on the pressures of public life and recalls meeting Marina in California. They discuss Marina's history of multiple marriages, including a union with actor Robert Trask and a marriage to playwright Isidore Wright, which resulted in a child. Mrs. Bantry suggests that Marina and her husband purchased the Hall to be closer to Elstree.
